prose:
Most electrophilic Fukui function (f+) for nitrobenzene, B3LYP/def2-TZVP gas-phase DFT calculation

Method provenance
Property: Atom-resolved Fukui reactivity indices (f+, f−, f⁰) and dual descriptor; headline value = largest electrophilic Fukui index (f+)
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard`  → B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted (PySCF backend)
Geometry provenance: single-point on user-supplied geometry (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz); no further optimisation (DFT//input-xyz)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation (+1, doublet) and anion (–1, doublet) states generated internally for the N ± 1 calculations
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (PySCF under the hood; version not specified in JSON)
Convergence & caveats: integrity.status = "ok", integrity.trustworthy = true. All SCF steps for neutral, cation, and anion states converged. Density fitting (RI/JK) used by default. Warnings reproduced below verbatim.

Result (headline number)
Most electrophilic atom: C4 (index 3, ring carbon ortho to NO₂) with f+ = 0.49641783 (dimensionless Mulliken-derived Fukui index).
